• 68 % of users avoid quick‑pay because amounts don’t match habit. | • ≈ 500 M active UPI users (incl. 350 M “regular” payers).
• Retail & utility merchants (bill‑pay, DTH, mobile recharge). | • One‑tap, personalized suggestions cut completion time → higher repeat usage.
• Lower error → fewer grievance tickets → higher NPS. | | **Demand‑side – Merchant & PSP revenue** | • Higher transaction frequency lifts interchange & value‑added‑service fees.
• Dynamic amounts enable upsell (e.g., “₹199 + ₹20 data pack”). | • Banks (HDFC, SBI, ICICI, Axis) – own UPI apps.
• Fintech PSPs (PhonePe, Google Pay, Paytm, Amazon Pay). | • Embedded analytics layer can be monetised as a “smart‑pay” add‑on (subscription or revenue‑share). | | **Supply‑side – Existing UPI API stack** | • UPI 2.0 (v2.0‑v2.2) already supports “suggested amount” field in the payment request payload.
• NPCI’s “UPI‑Analytics” sandbox enables real‑time transaction‑histogram queries. | • NPCI (infrastructure, standards).
• Bank‑level data lakes (core‑banking, RTGS‑API).
• Fintechs with ML pipelines (PhonePe, Google Pay). | • Leverage existing APIs → negligible latency overhead (< 30 ms).
• No new settlement flow – pure UI/UX layer. | | **Supply‑side – Analytics & ML capability** | • Lightweight clustering (top‑3 frequent amounts) runs in < 10 ms on cached user‑profile.
• Model can be served via NPCI’s “Analytics‑as‑a‑Service” (AaaS) platform. | • Bank data‑science teams (e.g., SBI’s AI hub).
• Fintech AI squads (PhonePe’s “PaySense”). | • Shared AaaS reduces per‑partner development cost to < ₹0.5 cr for rollout. | | **Regulatory anchor** | • RBI’s “Digital Payments Security Framework” (2023) mandates “minimum friction” and “privacy‑by‑design”.
• Guidelines allow on‑device inference for personalisation, provided data is pseudonymised. | • RBI (policy).
• NPCI (compliance certification). | • Feature is RBI‑aligned → faster approval, no fee‑structure changes. | **Bottom‑line:** - **Demand** is anchored on massive user friction (time & errors) and merchant appetite for higher frequency/ATV. - **Supply** is anchored on a mature UPI API, NPCI‑provided analytics services, and a regulatory environment that encourages friction‑less, privacy‑safe personalisation. - The convergence of these anchors makes a **nation‑wide, >10 M active‑user** rollout technically feasible and commercially compelling. ## Impact opportunity (users, delta in time, revenue) | Metric (3‑year horizon) | Year 1 | Year 2 | Year 3 | Assumptions | |------------------------|--------|--------|--------|--------------| | **Targeted active users** (who see suggestions) | 12 M | 28 M | 45 M | 12 % of total UPI base (≈ 500 M) adopt within 12 months; growth driven by bank/fintech roll‑outs. | | **Average time saved per transaction** | 2.4 s | 2.4 s | 2.4 s | Baseline manual entry 7.2 s → dynamic suggestion 4.8 s (static quick‑pay) → additional 2.4 s vs static. | | **Monthly transactions per active user** | 12 | 14 | 16 | Incremental 3‑5 % lift per quarter (cumulative 15 % by Y3). | | **Total time saved (seconds) per month** | 12 M × 12 × 2.4 ≈ 345 M s (≈ 96 k hrs) | 28 M × 14 × 2.4 ≈ 995 M s (≈ 276 k hrs) | 45 M × 16 × 2.4 ≈ 1.73 B s (≈ 480 k hrs) | Direct reduction in network latency pressure & support load. | | **Error‑rate reduction** | 0.4 % absolute (≈ 8 M fewer failed txns/mo) | 0.4 % absolute | 0.4 % absolute | From 2 % to 1.6 % error rate (RBI‑mandated “error‑free” target). | | **Revenue per active user (RPU)** – includes interchange, value‑added‑service share | ₹12 /mo | ₹15 /mo | ₹18 /mo | 25 % uplift from higher transaction frequency & upsell. | | **Incremental revenue (₹ crore) per month** | 12 M × ₹12 ≈ ₹144 cr | 28 M × ₹15 ≈ ₹420 cr | 45 M × ₹18 ≈ ₹810 cr | 3‑yr cumulative incremental revenue ≈ **₹1,374 crore**. | | **Net‑present‑value (5 % discount)** | – | – | **₹1,200 crore** | Conservative NPV over 3 yr. | ### Quick‑look at the business case - **Time‑savings** of ~480 k hrs/month by Y3 translates to **≈ ₹1,200 crore** in indirect cost avoidance (support ticket handling, churn mitigation). - **Error‑rate cut** saves ~8 M failed transactions/month → **₹200 cr** reduction in grievance‑related penalties and goodwill loss. - **Revenue lift** of **₹810 cr/month** by Y3 (≈ ₹9,720 cr/yr) dwarfs the estimated rollout cost (₹0.5 cr per partner integration × ~ 30 partners ≈ ₹15 cr). **Conclusion:** The dynamic amount‑suggestion layer can safely scale to >10 M users, deliver >2 seconds of time saved per transaction, and generate **₹1.2 trillion** of incremental value (direct revenue + cost avoidance) over three years while staying within existing UPI latency budgets and RBI compliance. This satisfies the scalability anchor of **>1 B monthly transactions** with sub‑30 ms added processing latency.

sections.title: 5. Validation
sections.content:
## Creating and operating MVP | Aspect | Details | |--------|---------| | **Scope of MVP** |
  • **Dynamic‑Amount‑Suggestion Service (DASS)** – a stateless micro‑service that reads the last 30 days of a user’s successful UPI transactions (via the existing `/transaction/history` API) and returns the top‑3 most frequent amounts for the selected merchant category.
  • **UI Hook** – a single‑tap “Suggested amount” carousel added to the payment screen of the pilot bank/PSP app (max 3 suggestions + editable field).
  • **Fraud & Privacy Guardrails** – rule‑engine that blocks suggestions > 2× the user’s average spend for that merchant, and logs consent‑checked data to the NPCI‑wide **Privacy‑Safe Store**.
| | **Who builds it** |
  • **NPCI Core Team** – product owner, API gateway extensions, DASS engine (Python/Go, containerised).
  • **Partner Bank Tech** – integrate UI hook, expose transaction‑history endpoint, run A/B test framework.
  • **FinTech/Analytics Vendor** – provide the lightweight ML model (frequency‑based, no personal identifiers) and monitoring dashboards.
| | **Pilot footprint** |
  • **Banks/PSPs** – 3 early adopters (one large public‑sector bank, one private‑sector bank, one neo‑bank).
  • **User base** – 5 million active UPI users (≈ 15 % of each bank’s digital‑pay user pool) for 30 days.
  • **Transaction volume target** – 200 million payment attempts (≈ 40 M suggested‑amount exposures).
| | **Operations** |
  • **Real‑time monitoring** – latency < 150 ms per suggestion, success‑rate > 99 %.
  • **Support model** – NPCI “Smart‑Pay” NOC (24 × 7) escalates incidents to partner banks via the existing **UPI Incident Management Portal**.
  • **Compliance** – RBI‑mandated audit logs (consent, suggestion generation, fraud‑check) stored for 5 years; periodic privacy‑impact review.
| --- ## Data it will generate to create insights | Data Stream | KPI (definition) | Expected Signal for Go/No‑Go | |-------------|------------------|------------------------------| | **Suggestion Acceptance Rate** | % of payments where a suggested amount is tapped (vs manual entry). | **≥ 15 %** acceptance → validates user relevance. | | **Transaction‑completion Time** | Avg. seconds from “Pay” tap to successful settlement. | **≥ 20 % reduction** vs static quick‑pay baseline (target ≤ 3.8 s). | | **Error / Failure Rate** | % of attempts that fail due to amount mismatch, insufficient funds, or fraud block. | **≤ 0.5 %** (must be lower than current 2 % manual‑entry error). | | **Repeat‑usage Lift** | % increase in repeat UPI transactions (same merchant, ≤ 30 days) for users exposed to suggestions. | **≥ 3 % quarterly lift** (aligned with research‑driven business case). | | **Average Transaction Value (ATV)** | Mean rupee value per completed payment. | **≥ 2 % uplift** over control group. | | **Revenue per Active User (RPAU)** | (Total interchange + value‑added‑service fees) ÷ number of active users in pilot. | **≥ ₹0.8** incremental RPAU (baseline ₹4.5). | | **Grievance Score** | Net promoter‑type score from post‑payment surveys (scale –100 to +100). | **No decline**; target ≥ +5 relative to control. | | **Fraud‑Vector Incidence** | Number of fraud alerts triggered by the suggestion engine per 1 M suggestions. | **≤ 0.1 %** (must stay within RBI‑defined risk tolerance). | | **Privacy‑Compliance Audits** | % of data‑access logs with valid consent flag. | **100 %** compliance. | ### How the data drives the go/no‑go decision 1. **Threshold Breach** – If any KPI falls below its minimum (e.g., acceptance < 15 % or error > 0.5 %), the pilot is paused for remediation. 2. **Positive Lift Confirmation** – Simultaneous achievement of ≥ 3 % repeat‑usage lift **and** ≥ 2 % ATV uplift constitutes a **“green light”** for full‑scale rollout. 3. **Risk Envelope** – Fraud‑vector incidence and privacy‑audit results must stay within RBI‑mandated limits; breach triggers a mandatory risk‑mitigation sprint before expansion. 4. **Economic Viability** – Incremental RPAU ≥ ₹0.8 combined with the operational cost model (≈ ₹0.12 per 1 M suggestions) yields a **> 5× ROI** – another go‑criterion. The MVP therefore serves as a data‑driven validation gate: only when the above quantitative signals are met will NPCI proceed to a nation‑wide launch, backed by a phased onboarding plan for all member banks and PSPs.

sections.title: 6. Product Operating
sections.content:
## 3 Success KPIs | # | KPI | Target | Measurement Horizon | |---|-----|--------|---------------------| | 1 | **Quarterly repeat‑UPI usage lift** (percentage of users who make ≥2 transactions in the same merchant‑category within 30 days) | **+3 % QoQ** (baseline Q2 2025 = 22 %) | End of Q4 2025 | | 2 | **Average payment‑completion time** (from QR‑scan / app‑open to “Success” status) | **‑20 %** vs static quick‑pay baseline (target ≤ 3.8 s) | 90 days post‑launch (mid‑Q3 2025) | | 3 | **Trust‑grievance rate** (grievances per 1 M successful transactions) | **‑30 %** (from 1.2 to ≤ 0.84) | 6 months after rollout (by end‑2025) | *All KPIs are tracked through NPCI’s existing analytics dashboards and reconciled with PSP‑level logs.* --- ## Grievance redressal (Trust) | Process | Detail | |---------|--------| | **Complaint capture** | UI‑level “Why did my suggestion fail?” button auto‑generates a ticket in the NPCI Grievance Management System (GMS) with transaction‑ID, suggested amount, and user comments. | | **Routing** | Tickets are auto‑routed to the originating PSP/Bank based on the VPA’s issuer. If the issue is system‑wide (e.g., suggestion service outage) it is escalated to NPCI Ops. | | **SLA** | *First acknowledgement*: ≤ 4 hrs.
*Resolution*:
‑ High‑severity (failed > ₹5 k or repeated failures) – ≤ 48 hrs.
‑ Low‑severity (single‑digit mismatch) – ≤ 72 hrs. | | **Escalation** | Unresolved tickets after SLA trigger an automatic escalation to the RBI‑mandated grievance escalation matrix and to the NPCI Trust & Safety Board. | | **Feedback loop** | Resolved tickets feed back into the suggestion‑engine model (error‑type tagging) within 24 hrs of closure. | --- ## Day 0 automation | Automated from launch | Manual / Human‑in‑the‑loop (initial) | |------------------------|--------------------------------------| | **Real‑time suggestion generation** – analytics engine reads last‑30‑day transaction histogram, emits top‑3 amounts via a low‑latency API (≤ 50 ms). | **Model tuning** – data‑science team reviews suggestion relevance weekly and adjusts feature weights. | | **Cache warm‑up** – pre‑populate suggestion cache for the top 5 % of active VPA‑merchant pairs. | **Exception handling** – PSP support teams manually review “suggestion‑failure” alerts flagged by the system. | | **Audit logging** – every suggestion request/response is logged to NPCI’s immutable ledger. | **Policy updates** – RBI/Bank compliance teams approve any change to suggestion‑display rules (e.g., max suggested amount). | | **Alerting** – auto‑generated alerts for latency > 100 ms or error‑rate > 0.2 % (sent to NPCI Ops). | **User education** – PSPs roll out in‑app tooltips and FAQs (manual content creation). | --- ## Impact on SGF (Settlement & Guarantee Fund) * **Reduced failed‑transaction volume** – error‑rate falls from 2 % to ≤ 1.4 % → ~0.6 % fewer settlement reversals per month. * **Lower SGF utilisation** – with fewer reversals, SGF drawdown is projected to drop by **≈ 0.5 % of monthly SGF outflow** (≈ ₹150 cr/month saved). * **Settlement latency unchanged** – core UPI settlement (2‑second batch) remains untouched; only the pre‑settlement suggestion layer adds a negligible (< 1 ms) overhead. --- ## Impact on FRM (Fraud & Risk Management) | Area | Impact | |------|--------| | **New fraud vector** – malicious actors could attempt “suggest‑tampering” (e.g., spoofed suggestion to over‑pay). | | **Mitigation** – FRM rule‑engine enriched with:
1. **Suggestion‑origin validation** (hash‑signed by NPCI analytics service).
2. **Amount‑deviation check** – flag if suggested amount deviates > 150 % from user’s historical mean. | | **Processing load** – FRM scoring calls increase by ~5 % (additional field in transaction payload). NPCI’s risk‑engine autoscaling already supports up to 1.2 B txn/month, so capacity headroom is sufficient. | | **Detection benefit** – early‑stage suggestion‑mismatch alerts improve fraud‑catch rate by an estimated **+2 %** (additional ₹2 cr/month recovered). | --- ## Impact on existing txns and infra | Dimension | Change | |-----------|--------| | **Transaction flow** – a new **GET /suggest‑amount** call is inserted after VPA lookup and before user confirmation. The call returns up to three amounts and a confidence score. | | **Latency** – added API latency ≤ 50 ms (target 95th‑percentile). Overall end‑to‑end payment time improves by ~20 % vs static quick‑pay. | | **Volume** – projected uplift of **+3 %** in transaction count translates to **≈ 30 M extra txn/month** (baseline 1 B). Existing settlement pipelines already handle > 2 B/month, so no scaling required. | | **Infrastructure** – deployment of a horizontally‑scalable **Suggestion Service** (Kubernetes, autoscaling, 3‑zone redundancy). Required compute: 120 vCPU, 256 GB RAM for peak 1 B monthly requests – fits within NPCI’s current cloud‑budget envelope. | | **Data storage** – incremental storage of 30‑day transaction aggregates per VPA (~5 TB total) – provisioned on existing HDFS cluster with 20 % headroom. | | **Backward compatibility** – PSPs that do not adopt the API continue to receive the current static quick‑pay list; the suggestion service is opt‑in via a version flag in the UPI‑API contract. | ---

sections.title: 7. Product Comms (external + internal)
sections.content:
## Product demo (polished version of MVP)  
**Format** – Interactive, web‑based sandbox hosted on NPCI’s developer portal (https://developer.npci.org).  
**Target audience** –  

- Bank & PSP product owners (UX, digital banking)  
- Fintech partners (payment aggregators, wallet providers)  
- Select merchant API integrators (large‑format retail, DTH, utility)  

**Key scenarios to showcase**  

| # | Scenario | Flow highlights | Success metric shown |
|---|----------|----------------|----------------------|
| 1 | **Repeat bill‑pay** – user recharges mobile after a week | System pulls last‑30‑day spend, surfaces “₹199 (₹199 + ₹5 discount)”, one‑tap confirm | 45 % reduction in entry time vs manual entry |
| 2 | **Merchant‑specific suggestion** – grocery checkout | Suggests “₹499 (average basket)”, editable field pre‑filled | 2 % lower error‑rate (no mistyped digits) |
| 3 | **Cross‑sell trigger** – post‑pay‑bill, UI shows “Add ₹99 (OTT subscription) ” | Real‑time recommendation engine, opt‑in via single tap | +3 % lift in ancillary transaction volume |
| 4 | **Fallback path** – user edits suggested amount | Editable field retains suggestion chip, validates against UPI limits | 100 % compliance with RBI amount‑range rules |
| 5 | **Analytics dashboard** – bank admin view | Shows suggestion‑acceptance %, avg. time saved, fraud‑alert flags | Immediate KPI visibility for partners |

Demo runs in **5 minutes** (incl. Q&A) and ends with a **live API call trace** (cURL) to illustrate the new `suggested_amounts` field in the UPI request payload.

---

## Product video  
**Purpose** – External awareness & partner enablement (marketing + technical onboarding).  
**Length** – 2 min 30 sec (concise for social & email).  
**Structure**  

1. **Problem (15 s)** – Quick stats: 7.2 s avg. manual entry, 2 % error rate.  
2. **Solution (30 s)** – Animation of dynamic suggestion chip appearing on the payment screen.  
3. **Live demo snippet (45 s)** – Real‑world user flow (mobile recharge, grocery).  
4. **Benefits (30 s)** – 20 % extra speed, 3‑5 % quarterly repeat‑usage lift, compliance badge.  
5. **Ecosystem call‑to‑action (30 s)** – “Add the `suggested_amounts` field via API v2.2, start personalising today.”  
6. **Closing (10 s)** – NPCI logo, RBI compliance note, contact link.

**Target audience** – Bank digital heads, fintech product teams, merchant aggregators, press & analyst community.

---

## Explanation video by PM  
**Audience** – Internal stakeholders (NPCI product, engineering, legal, compliance) and external partner technical leads.  
**Format** – Screen‑recorded walkthrough with voice‑over (≈ 12 min).  
**Key messages**  

- **Why** we built it: data‑driven friction reduction, RBI “secure, efficient” mandate.  
- **How** the suggestion engine works: transaction histogram, ML scoring, privacy‑by‑design.  
- **API contract**: new `suggested_amounts` array, optional `confidence_score`, fallback rules.  
- **Risk controls**: fraud‑vector checks, rate‑limiting, data‑masking per RBI GDPR‑like guidelines.  
- **Roll‑out plan**: sandbox → pilot (2 banks, 3 fintechs) → production (Q4 2026).  

**Distribution** – Private YouTube link shared on NPCI Confluence, embedded in partner onboarding portal; transcript uploaded for searchable reference.

---

## FAQs + trained LLM  
### Top 5 FAQs  

| # | Question | Concise answer |
|---|----------|----------------|
| 1 | *What new field do I need to add to my UPI request?* | Add `suggested_amounts` (array of objects `{amount, label, confidence}`) under the `payment` node; optional, backward‑compatible. |
| 2 | *Will the suggestions expose user transaction history?* | No. Suggestions are generated on‑device or via NPCI’s anonymised analytics service; only aggregate confidence is returned. |
| 3 | *How does this affect my fraud‑monitoring rules?* | New `suggested_amounts` are flagged with a `source=dynamic`; existing AML/KYC checks remain unchanged. |
| 4 | *What is the latency impact?* | < 150 ms added on average (cached histogram + lightweight scoring). |
| 5 | *Do I need RBI approval to enable the feature?* | No separate approval; the feature complies with RBI’s “Digital Payments – Security & Efficiency” circular (see NPCI Circular). |

### LLM Training Plan  

1. **Data collection** – Pull all UPI‑API documentation, NPCI circulars, and the above FAQ set (≈ 2 k Q‑A pairs).  
2. **Domain fine‑tuning** – Use OpenAI’s `gpt‑4o-mini` base, fine‑tune on the curated corpus (≈ 5 epochs).  
3. **Safety layer** – Integrate RBI‑compliant content filter (keywords: “KYC”, “AML”, “privacy”).  
4. **Evaluation** – Run 500 synthetic queries; target ≥ 92 % exact‑match accuracy on FAQ set.  
5. **Deployment** – Host as a REST endpoint (`/v1/assist`) behind NPCI’s API gateway; enable partner access via API‑key authentication.  

The LLM will be the first line of support for partner developers, reducing ticket volume by an estimated **30 %** during rollout.

---

## Circular  
**Title** – *NPCI Circular No. 2026‑03: “Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion Layer for UPI Transactions”*  

| Element | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| **Scope** | All banks, PSPs, and third‑party aggregators that implement UPI v2.2 or later. |
| **Mandatory requirements** | 1. Support the `suggested_amounts` field in inbound/outbound UPI requests. 2. Implement RBI‑mandated data‑privacy safeguards (data minimisation, encryption at rest & in transit). 3. Log suggestion‑acceptance events for audit (minimum 90 days). |
| **Optional enhancements** | Real‑time confidence score, merchant‑category‑specific chips, A/B‑testing flags. |
| **Compliance deadline** | **30 Sept 2026** for production rollout; sandbox compliance by **31 Mar 2026**. |
| **Reporting** | Quarterly compliance report to NPCI (template provided) + incident‑response SLA ≤ 4 hrs for suggestion‑related fraud alerts. |
| **Enforcement** | Non‑compliant entities will be placed on “restricted‑functionality” list, limiting access to UPI‑API v2.2 features. |

The circular will be disseminated via NPCI’s official mailing list, posted on the developer portal, and referenced in the partner onboarding checklist.

---

## Product doc (w/ Specs, explanations, test cases/UI/UX guidelines)  
**Repository** – Confluence space `UPI‑Dynamic‑Suggestion` (owner: Product Lead, custodians: Architecture, QA, Compliance).  

| Section | Content | Owner |
|---------|---------|-------|
| **1. Overview** | Business case, KPI targets, regulatory alignment. | Product Management |
| **2. API Specification** | JSON schema for `suggested_amounts`, request/response examples, versioning matrix. | Architecture |
| **3. Data Flow & Privacy Model** | Diagram of data extraction, anonymisation, on‑device scoring, GDPR‑like safeguards. | Security & Compliance |
| **4. UI/UX Guidelines** | Design tokens for suggestion chips, accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA), error handling, fallback UI. | Design System |
| **5. Integration Guide** | Step‑by‑step for sandbox → production, SDK snippets (Android, iOS, Web), rate‑limit tables. | Engineering |
| **6. Test Cases** | Functional (suggestion accuracy ≥ 80 %), performance (≤ 150 ms latency), security (data‑leak tests), regression matrix. | QA |
| **7. Monitoring & Analytics** | KPI dashboards (acceptance %, avg. time saved), alert thresholds, log schema. | Operations |
| **8. FAQ & Support** | Expanded FAQ list, LLM endpoint details, escalation matrix. | Support |
| **9. Change Log & Versioning** | Release notes, deprecation policy. | Release Management |

All sections are version‑controlled; updates trigger a **bi‑weekly review** and an automated **CI lint** for schema consistency. The final document will be exported as a PDF for RBI audit submission.

sections.title: 9. Potential Risks
sections.content:
## Fraud risk  
- **Synthetic‑identity suggestion abuse** – a fraudster could manipulate the suggestion engine (e.g., by creating a “ghost” transaction history through low‑value test payments) to push high‑value amounts that the victim later approves with a single tap.  
  - *Mitigation*: cap suggested amounts to the **90th percentile of the user’s last 30 days spend** per merchant category; require a **re‑authentication (PIN/biometric)** for any suggestion exceeding the user’s historic average + 2 σ.  
- **Replay/relay attacks on suggestion payload** – the suggestion list is transmitted from the UPI app to the PSP via the UPI Intent flow; an attacker could intercept and replace it with inflated amounts.  
  - *Mitigation*: sign the suggestion payload with the **PSP’s private key** and verify the signature on the NPCI gateway; enforce **TLS 1.3** end‑to‑end encryption.  
- **Social‑engineering “tap‑and‑pay”** – because the UI reduces friction, users may be more prone to approve a malicious suggestion sent via phishing (e.g., a spoofed merchant QR).  
  - *Mitigation*: display the **merchant name and amount** prominently; add a **one‑time visual cue** (e.g., “Suggested for you”) that disappears after 30 seconds; educate users via NPCI’s grievance‑reduction campaign.  

---

## Infosec risk  
- **Exposure of transaction histograms** – the analytics layer must store per‑user frequency tables (amount, merchant‑category, time‑of‑day). If breached, attackers gain insight into spending patterns.  
  - *Mitigation*: store histograms in **encrypted-at‑rest** vaults (AES‑256 GCM); rotate encryption keys every 90 days; limit access to **role‑based service accounts** with MFA.  
- **In‑flight data leakage** – suggestion requests travel from the UPI app → PSP → NPCI → downstream merchant.  
  - *Mitigation*: enforce **mutual TLS** between all parties; use **payload‑level signing** (JWS) and **token‑binding** to tie the request to the originating device.  
- **Denial‑of‑service on analytics service** – a surge in suggestion calls could overload the ML micro‑service, causing latency spikes that breach the **≤ 200 ms** response SLA.  
  - *Mitigation*: implement **rate‑limiting (max 5 suggestions / sec per user)**, auto‑scale via container orchestration, and fallback to static quick‑pay buttons if latency > 250 ms.  

---

## Legal risk  
- **Contractual breach with PSPs** – PSPs may argue that dynamic suggestions alter the “payment initiation” definition under the **UPI API Specification v2.0**, potentially triggering penalty clauses.  
  - *Management*: embed the suggestion step as a **pre‑authorization UI** (not a transaction) and obtain explicit consent in the PSP‑to‑NPCI SLA amendment.  
- **RBI compliance – “Data localisation”** – RBI mandates that all payment‑related data be stored on servers **within India**.  
  - *Management*: host the analytics engine in **NPCI‑approved data centres** (e.g., NSDL, NIC) and certify under **RBI’s IT Framework for Payments**.  
- **Consumer protection – “Unfair trade practice”** – if suggestions consistently push higher amounts, regulators could deem it coercive.  
  - *Management*: enforce a **maximum uplift rule** (suggested amount ≤ 150 % of user’s median spend for that merchant) and disclose the algorithmic basis in the user‑terms.  

---

## Data privacy risk  
- **Personal data processed** – UPI VPA, transaction timestamps, merchant category codes, amount buckets, device identifier (IMEI/Android ID).  
- **Privacy concerns** – profiling could be repurposed for targeted advertising, violating **RBI’s Privacy Framework** and **IT (Reasonable Security Practices) Rules, 2011**.  
- **Controls**  
  - **Purpose limitation** – use data **solely** for “payment‑flow optimisation”; log any secondary use and obtain explicit opt‑in.  
  - **Data minimisation** – retain only aggregated histograms (no raw transaction IDs) for **30 days**; purge thereafter.  
  - **User consent & transparency** – present a concise consent screen (“We will use your last 30 days spend to suggest amounts”) with a link to the **NPCI Privacy Notice**.  
  - **Differential privacy** – add calibrated noise to frequency counts when serving suggestions to mitigate re‑identification risk.  

---

## 2nd order negative effect  
- **Reduced manual amount entry leading to lower merchant price‑negotiation** – merchants may lose the “up‑sell” opportunity that occurs when users manually type a higher amount (e.g., paying ₹1,200 instead of the suggested ₹1,000).  
  - *Mitigation*: allow merchants to **opt‑in** to a “suggest‑plus” mode that adds a **recommended tip or surcharge** (capped at 5 % per RBI guidelines).  
- **Algorithmic lock‑in** – users may become dependent on the suggested amounts, limiting their ability to discover better deals or alternate merchants, potentially stifling competition.  
  - *Mitigation*: rotate suggestions **daily** and surface an “Other amount” button that opens the full numeric keypad.  
- **Systemic bias** – if the training data over‑represents high‑spend users, low‑income users may receive suggestions that are too high, increasing failed transactions and grievance rates.  
  - *Mitigation*: segment models by **income‑proxy buckets** (e.g., transaction frequency) and enforce a **maximum suggestion ceiling** of ₹5,000 for the lowest segment.  

---  

*All risk statements are grounded in current RBI circulars (e.g., RBI/2024/44 on “Data Security in Digital Payments”) and NPCI’s technical standards (UPI API v2.0, 2025). Continuous monitoring via the NPCI Risk‑Ops Dashboard will ensure that any deviation from the above controls triggers an automated escalation.*

sections.title: 10. Compliance
sections.content:
## Existing guideline change **RBI / NPCI references that must be revised** | Existing Guideline | Current Text (relevant excerpt) | Required Amendment | Rationale for Change | |--------------------|--------------------------------|--------------------|----------------------| | **RBI Master Direction – “Regulation on Payment and Settlement Systems” (Mar 2023)** – Clause 4.2.1 (Data usage) | “Payment system operators shall use transaction data only for settlement, reconciliation and statutory reporting.” | Expand to permit **non‑settlement analytics** for “personalised amount‑suggestion” provided:
• User‑level consent is obtained (per RBI Data Privacy Framework).
• Data is anonymised/aggregated where possible. | The smart‑suggest feature needs to mine the last‑30‑day spend histogram in real‑time. Without an explicit carve‑out, such analytics would breach the current “settlement‑only” restriction. | | **RBI Circular – “Guidelines for Payment Aggregators / Payment Gateways” (Oct 2022)** – Annex B (Transaction limits) | “Aggregators may not alter the transaction amount entered by the payer.” | Add a **“Dynamic Suggestion Exception”** allowing the UI to pre‑populate a suggested amount, but the payer must retain the ability to edit before authorisation. | Ensures the feature is treated as a UI aid, not a system‑initiated amount change, keeping it within the regulator’s risk appetite. | | **NPCI UPI API Specification v2.0 (released Jan 2025)** – Section 5.3 (Quick‑Pay Buttons) | “Only static quick‑pay amounts (₹100, ₹500, ₹1000) are supported.” | Introduce a new **`/suggestedAmounts`** endpoint that returns a list of up to three personalised amounts per payer‑merchant pair. | Provides a formal technical contract for banks/PSPs to implement the feature without breaching existing API contracts. | | **RBI Circular – “Cyber Security Framework for Payment Systems” (Feb 2024)** – Clause 6.1 (Logging) | “All transaction‑level logs must be retained for a minimum of 180 days.” | Extend retention to **360 days for analytics logs** (suggestion generation, consent logs) and mandate **tamper‑evident storage** (e.g., hash‑chained logs). | Analytics logs are essential for audit of consent and fraud‑vector monitoring for the new feature. | --- ## New guideline addition **Circulars / Guidelines that need to be introduced** | New Guideline (Proposed Title) | Issuing Authority | Core Coverage | |-------------------------------|-------------------|---------------| | **RBI “Guidelines on Personalised Payment Experience & Data‑Driven UI Enhancements”** | RBI – Department of Payment & Settlement Systems | • **User Consent Model** – explicit opt‑in/opt‑out via UPI app UI; consent record stored for 5 years.
• **Data Minimisation** – only last‑30 days of successful payer‑merchant transactions, no PII beyond VPA.
• **Purpose Limitation** – data used solely for amount‑suggestion; any secondary use requires separate consent.
• **Security Controls** – encryption at rest (AES‑256) and in‑flight (TLS 1.3); secure enclave for ML inference.
• **Audit & Reporting** – quarterly compliance report to RBI on suggestion accuracy, false‑positive fraud triggers, and grievance metrics. | | **NPCI “Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion Framework (DASF) – Product Circular”** | NPCI – UPI Operations Committee | • **API Specification** – `/suggestedAmounts` request/response schema, latency SLA ≤ 150 ms.
• **Performance & Load** – must support ≥ 1 billion monthly suggestion calls with ≤ 2 % error rate.
• **Fraud‑Vector Controls** – real‑time risk score check before suggestion is sent; disallow suggestions for high‑risk merchants (as per RBI’s “Risk‑Based Segmentation”).
• **Grievance Handling** – any dispute arising from a suggested amount must be logged and resolved within 48 hours; escalation matrix defined.
• **Testing & Certification** – mandatory sandbox testing; certification by NPCI’s “Security & Compliance Lab” before production rollout. | | **RBI “Standardised Grievance Redressal SLA for UI‑Assisted Payments”** | RBI – Consumer Protection Cell | • **SLA** – 95 % of complaints on suggested‑amount mismatches resolved within 24 hrs; 99 % within 72 hrs.
• **Metrics** – monthly grievance‑rate ≤ 0.05 % of total suggestion‑enabled transactions.
• **Reporting** – aggregated grievance statistics to be submitted to RBI and NPCI on a monthly basis. | --- ## Must have compliances in NPCI product circular for ecosystem **Mandatory obligations that every ecosystem participant (banks, PSPs, fintechs, aggregators, and app providers) must fulfil to enable the Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion feature** 1. **Consent Management** - Capture explicit opt‑in from the payer before any suggestion is generated. - Store consent artefacts (timestamp, VPA, version of consent text) for a minimum of **5 years**. - Provide a UI toggle for opt‑out that is honoured in real‑time. 2. **Data Privacy & Minimisation** - Use only **successful transaction amount, merchant category code (MCC), and timestamp** for the last 30 days. - No storage of full transaction narratives, location data, or personal identifiers beyond the VPA. - Apply **pseudonymisation** for any intermediate analytics processing. 3. **Security Controls** - All suggestion‑related API calls must be over **TLS 1.3** with mutual authentication (client certificates). - Encryption of analytics data at rest with **AES‑256**;
